footer.mission: Plain-language digital education. Free to share, built to be translated.

home.title: Digital know-how, one byte at a time.
home.lede: Free five-minute lessons on data privacy, tech literacy, and digital inclusion — written in plain language and built to travel across languages.
home.topicPrivacyBody: Who can see what you do online, and the small habits that keep your accounts and identity yours.
home.topicLiteracyBody: How the everyday internet actually works, so headlines, scams, and settings stop being mysteries.
home.topicInclusionBody: Why millions are still offline or underserved, and what communities can do about it.
home.bandBody: Every lesson is written to be translated, adapted, and taught anywhere. Help us carry it further — translate a lesson, write one, or run a workshop in your community.

about.title: Education that travels like a letter.
about.lede: Bytes Without Borders is a volunteer initiative informing the broader population through localized micro-lessons — short, practical, and free.
about.missionBody1: Most guides about staying safe and capable online are long, technical, and written in one language for one audience. The people with the most to lose — new internet users, older adults, communities getting connected for the first time — rarely get materials written for them.
about.missionBody2: So we keep every lesson under five minutes, in plain language, free of jargon and fear. Like a correspondence course, each one is designed to be passed along: translated, printed, taught out loud, or read on a cheap phone over a slow connection.
about.how1Body: One idea per lesson, everyday words, real examples — no acronyms without introductions.
about.how2Body: Volunteers with security and education backgrounds check every claim before it ships.
about.how3Body: Lessons avoid culture-bound references so translators adapt meaning, not just words.
about.value1: Digital skills are civic skills — everyone deserves them, in their own language.
about.value2: Clarity is respect. If a lesson confuses its reader, the lesson is wrong.
about.value3: Knowledge should be free to share, adapt, and teach onward.

involved.title: Carry a lesson further.
involved.lede: Everything here is made by volunteers. Pick the way that fits the time and skills you have.
involved.way1Body: Bring a lesson into your language. Our lessons avoid idioms and culture-bound examples, so a careful translation usually takes under an hour.
involved.way2Body: Know a topic well? Draft a five-minute lesson in plain words. Volunteer reviewers help you fact-check and tighten it.
involved.way3Body: Run a lesson at a library, community center, or kitchen table. Every lesson works printed, projected, or read aloud.
involved.formMessage: How would you like to help?
involved.formNote: The form opens your email app — or write to us directly at

hub.title: Every lesson, five minutes or less.
hub.lede: Short, practical, and jargon-free. Read them in any order — each one stands alone.

notfound.title: Lost in transit.
notfound.body: This page could not be delivered — it may have moved or never existed. The lessons are all still where they should be.
